mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2.0% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.7% as of May 2018 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor market and wage pressure that constrains corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% as of May 2018 indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0%, or correlation between UNRATE and forward returns becomes positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 3% year-over-year while housing starts remain flat, signaling weakening consumer demand that portends earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate falls below 3% YoY AND HOUST shows zero or negative monthly change
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 501971M and HOUST at 1287K as of April 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month while remaining above 107.0, indicating supply-side softening that reduces earnings growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO shows negative monthly change while level remains above 107.0
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 200K per month while unemployment remains below 4.0%, signaling overheating labor market that triggers Fed tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 200K AND UNRATE < 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148662K and UNRATE at 3.8% as of May 2018
regime_hint: late-cycle overheating with Fed tightening bias
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth remains above 2.5% annualized while housing starts exceed 1300K units, signaling robust demand momentum that supports equity earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days in expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17379.7B and HOUST at 1287K as of January 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances despite both conditions met, or Rank IC of joint signal < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, creating negative real rates that signal monetary policy lag and market expectations of future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 250.535 and FEDFUNDS at 1.7% as of May 2018
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.018 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production accelerates above 108.0 index level while payroll employment growth sustains above 150K monthly, signaling robust supply-side expansion that supports earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days in mid-cycle regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.3 and PAYEMS at 148662K as of May 2018
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 58% of co-triggered instances, or Rank IC of joint signal < 0.012 over any 18-month rolling window.
